name: Dropdown Panel
description: A hierarchical dropdown list for selecting items with optional search, grouping, and multiple selection modes.

anatomy.name: SelectHeaderRow
anatomy.description: Top row containing a select-all checkbox icon and a search input field, 8px vertical padding, 8px gap

anatomy.name: SearchInput
anatomy.description: Text input with magnifying glass icon for filtering, 12px horizontal padding, 6px gap, 8px corner radius, 1px border

anatomy.name: CheckboxRadioIcon
anatomy.description: Selection indicator, checkbox for multi-select, radio for single-select

anatomy.name: RowLabel
anatomy.description: Primary text content of each dropdown row, truncates with ellipsis

anatomy.name: GroupParent
anatomy.description: Bold (weight 600) category row with a caret icon, used when nesting is enabled

anatomy.name: GroupChild
anatomy.description: Indented row nested under a group parent, indented via left padding (56px medium, 48px small)

anatomy.name: CaretIcon
anatomy.description: Chevron on group parent rows indicating expandable/collapsible state, 16x16, 1.5 stroke width

anatomy.name: Separator
anatomy.description: Horizontal divider line between groups of items

do: Use multi-select when users need to pick several items
Use single-select when only one choice is valid
Enable nesting for hierarchical data like locations or categories
Show the search header when the list contains more than 7 items
Use the small size in compact layouts like sidebars, popovers, or canvas areas
dont: Don't use for flat lists with fewer than 5 items, use checkboxes or radios instead
Don't nest more than 2 levels deep
Don't hide the header when search is essential for discoverability
Don't mix multi and single selection within the same instance
